commit.message:
Add Node.js-based JavaScript cell execution

Route JavaScript cells to a Node.js subprocess instead of the
dotnet-interactive kernel (which has no server-side JS support).
NodeJsExecutor writes cell code to a temp file, runs it via 'node',
captures stdout/stderr, and maps results into cell outputs.

ExecutionCoordinator now routes JS cells through NodeJsExecutor while
all other languages continue through the kernel. Supports Run Cell,
Run All, Run Selection, and Run Cells Above/Below.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix orphaned dotnet-interactive processes on VS shutdown

Add a static process registry to KernelProcessManager so the package
can dispose all living instances at shutdown. Previously, cleanup
only happened per-editor-pane — if VS exited without individually
disposing every open pane (crash, force-quit, or disposal ordering),
the kernel processes would survive as orphans.

- KernelProcessManager: add ConcurrentDictionary-based instance
  tracking (register in ctor, unregister in Dispose) and static
  DisposeAll() method
- PolyglotNotebooksPackage: override Dispose(bool) to call
  KernelProcessManager.DisposeAll() on package teardown

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Variable Explorer command being unresponsive when clicked

Root Cause: Community.VisualStudio.Toolkit's BaseToolWindow<T> requires explicit
Initialize(ToolkitPackage) call during package initialization. The toolkit has no
auto-discovery mechanism. Without the Initialize call, ShowAsync() throws
InvalidOperationException that the command infrastructure silently swallows.

Changes:
- PolyglotNotebooksPackage.InitializeAsync(): Added VariableExplorerToolWindow.Initialize(this)
- ShowVariableExplorerCommand.cs: Added diagnostic logging for command invocation
- VariableExplorerToolWindow.cs: Added diagnostic logging for tool window creation
- Added rule: All new BaseToolWindow<T> subclasses must have explicit Initialize calls

Documentation:
- Decision 12 appended to decisions.md with root cause and pattern for future tool windows
- Wendy's orchestration log created
- Session log created
- Cross-agent notifications sent to Penny, Ralph, and Ellie

Build: 0 errors
Verification: Command now responds to clicks, tool window displays correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

squad: Merge toolbar & kernel fixes (2026-03-28T21:20Z)

Merged two maintenance fixes:

- Wendy (UI Specialist): Fixed status text colors (VizSurfaceGoldMediumKey → ToolWindowTextKey) and reordered toolbar to put status left of execution counter. Impact: Status text now visible on light/dark themes; improved toolbar information flow.

- Ellie (Editor Specialist): Fixed KernelInfoCache fallback list — removed non-built-in kernels (javascript, typescript, sql, markdown) that caused NoSuitableKernelException. Fallback now contains only guaranteed built-in kernels (csharp, fsharp, pwsh, html). Dynamic population via KernelReady still works.

Updated decisions.md with Decision 10 (ToolWindowTextKey for status text) and Decision 11 (Kernel fallback list). Merged decision inbox, updated agent histories.

Build: 0 errors, 309 tests passing.

commit.message:
fix: use 127.0.0.1 in healthcheck to avoid IPv6 resolution failure

Node.js listens on IPv4 (0.0.0.0) but 'localhost' resolves to [::1] in
Alpine, causing the healthcheck wget to get 'Connection refused'.

commit.message:
Auth-aware SSR: Remove window check to enable JWT in server-side requests

Problem: axios interceptor had 'if (typeof window !== undefined)' check that
skipped auth during server-side renders. Result: prefetch returned hasVoted: false
for all users. Client-side auth loads after React Query hydration, too late to
update state. This caused heart buttons to show wrong state + required query
invalidation workarounds that flicker and cause extra fetches.

Solution: Remove the window check. The Supabase SSR client (createBrowserClient)
automatically handles cookies on both client and server contexts. Now:
- Server-side prefetch requests include JWT via cookies
- Prefetched data has correct hasVoted state from page load
- No flicker, no extra fetches, no query invalidation needed

Changes:
- custom-instance.ts: Remove 'typeof window' check to enable SSR auth
- Removed 5 query invalidation useEffects (now unnecessary):
  * tips-tab.tsx
  * user-tips-infinite-tab.tsx
  * user-scams-infinite-tab.tsx
  * gallery-tab.tsx
  * search-results-tabs.tsx

Impact: All voteable content (tips, gallery, scams, spots) automatically
inherit the fix. No changes needed to components, hooks, or query keys.

Build: ✓ Compiles successfully (pre-existing TypeScript error in generated code unrelated to this change)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Hashtable vs PSObject: add -OutputType PSObject to all Invoke-MgGraphRequest calls

Invoke-MgGraphRequest returns Hashtable by default; dot-notation
property access (e.g. \.'@odata.nextLink', \.file, \.photo)
only works on PSCustomObject. Adding -OutputType PSObject fixes all
dot-access crashes throughout the script including '@odata.nextLink'.
Also simplified Read-OneDriveJson to use PSObject path directly.

commit.message:
fix: use ld-linux-armhf.so.3 for hard-float ARM, bootstrap symlink

Port of fix/arm-dynlinker to main (/opt prefix).

Bug 1: rules.mk set DYNLINKER=ld-linux.so.3 for all ARM. Hard-float
(gnueabihf) requires ld-linux-armhf.so.3. Fix adds eabihf check via
TARGET_SUFFIX.

Bug 2: ldd sed in package/libs/toolchain/Makefile was missing the
ld-linux-armhf.so.3 → /opt/lib/ld-linux-armhf.so.3 substitution.

Bug 3: generated generic.sh missing bootstrap symlink before first opkg
run. Added ln -sf /lib/${LOADER} /opt/lib/${LOADER} so opkg can use the
host glibc loader before Entware's glibc is installed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove pnpm-setup duplicate cache + add cache-cleanup reusable workflow (#70)

* Initial plan

* fix: pnpm-setup duplicate cache + add cache-cleanup reusable workflow

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/kryota-dev/actions/sessions/aa500c6d-bebc-4ee5-aeba-433a8ff528fd

Co-authored-by: kryota-dev <50436249+kryota-dev@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cache-cleanup): address review feedback for robustness

- Add positive integer validation for max-age-days input
- Consolidate jq calls into single @tsv extraction per cache entry
- Add null/empty guard for last_accessed_at with warning skip
- Wrap DELETE call to continue on failure instead of aborting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cache-cleanup): separate collection and deletion to avoid pagination skips

Refactor the cache cleanup loop into two phases:
- Phase 1: Paginate and collect all stale cache entries into an array
- Phase 2: Delete collected entries sequentially

This prevents the issue where deleting caches during pagination causes
remaining entries to shift, potentially skipping stale caches on
subsequent pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cache-cleanup): use tab-only IFS for TSV parsing in collection phase

Set IFS=$'\t' on the read command that parses jq @tsv output during
cache collection. Without this, cache keys containing spaces would
be split incorrectly, causing date parsing failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: kryota-dev <50436249+kryota-dev@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: Hindu Festival Photo Album Creator for OneDrive

- FestivalAlbums.ps1: PowerShell script that scans OneDrive photos,
  matches them to Hindu festival dates via Calendarific API, and creates
  named albums (e.g. DiwaliLifetime) using OneDrive bundles
- Supports resume after interruption via OneDrive-stored state file
- Configurable festival list, rescan mode, and checkpoint frequency
- README.md with setup guide and usage instructions

commit.message:
Pure CSS responsive grid layout

Replace JS layout optimizer with pure CSS using minmax columns
and aspect-ratio media queries. Buttons fill width on narrow
screens and are height-capped + centered on wide screens.
